violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC has enrolled in course and will register with RIDOH CFP once completed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

Cooked onions were observed held at 97 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff took to reheat to 165 degrees during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024

Raw animal foods (raw shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (prepared vegetables) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

Cut tomatoes and lettuce were observed held at 78- and 80- degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Containers of beans, pork, and meatballs were observed cooling on the counter in plastic containers more than 4 inches deep. Foods were relocated to refrigerator for active cooling. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2024

The presence of roaches is not controlled as evidenced by roach carcasses and feces. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2024
The open window and door in the kitchen do not have screens or other covering to prevent the entrance of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024
Single use containers are being reused to store foods. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024
The following were not in good repair: -door to walk-in observed with hole and exposed insulation -gaskets to 3-door reach-in. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

The condensate from the walk-in and the 3-door reach-in refrigeration units are draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024
Inspector observed a hole in the floor/wall juncture under the 3-bay sink in the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement and outdoor seating area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/24/2024
